title: PwD Registration by Caregiver (SwarajAbility Access) - Functional Test Plan
overview: Comprehensive functional testing of the PwD (Person with Disability) registration feature by caregiver on the AT/AD platform. This feature enables caregivers to register individuals with disabilities after completing their own registration and providing consent, granting access to both AT/AD platform and parent platform SwarajAbility. The test plan covers registration flow, consent management, dual-platform access, relationship management, and multi-PwD registration scenarios.

inScope: PwD profile creation and data collection

testCases.title: Verify PwD registration is accessible only after caregiver registration completion
testCases.preconditions: An unregistered user account exists; Caregiver registration page is accessible
steps: 1. Navigate to caregiver registration page
2. Attempt to access PwD registration before completing caregiver registration
3. Verify access status/visibility of PwD registration option
4. Complete caregiver registration with all mandatory fields
5. Verify PwD registration option becomes available after registration completion
6. Navigate to PwD registration section
7. Verify PwD registration form is accessible and functional
expectedResults: PwD registration option is NOT visible/accessible before caregiver registration
After caregiver registration, confirmation page displays success message
PwD registration becomes accessible and viewable in caregiver's dashboard
PwD registration form loads without errors
testCases.postconditions: Caregiver account is created and active; PwD registration form is ready for input

testCases.title: Verify PwD registration requires explicit caregiver consent
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is registered and logged in; Caregiver has not yet provided PwD registration consent
steps: 1. Navigate to PwD registration page as registered caregiver
2. Locate consent disclosure/checkbox section
3. Attempt to proceed with PwD registration without checking consent box
4. Verify system prevents form submission
5. Read consent terms displayed on page
6. Check the consent checkbox to agree to terms
7. Verify form submission is now possible
8. Continue with PwD registration form completion
expectedResults: Consent section is clearly visible with explicit checkbox
Form submission is blocked with error message when consent unchecked: 'You must agree to the terms to proceed'
Consent checkbox is clearly labeled with full terms visible
Form submission is enabled after consent is checked
User can proceed to next step of registration
testCases.postconditions: Caregiver consent is recorded; PwD registration form is ready for profile data entry

testCases.title: Verify successful PwD profile creation with complete information
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is registered and logged in; Caregiver has provided consent; PwD registration form is accessible
steps: 1. Navigate to PwD registration form
2. Enter complete PwD profile information (name, age, disability type, phone, email)
3. Select disability category from dropdown
4. Enter PwD email address for account creation
5. Set secure password for PwD account
6. Confirm password entry
7. Review entered information for accuracy
8. Click 'Register PwD' or 'Submit' button
9. Wait for processing completion
10. Verify confirmation message
expectedResults: All mandatory fields accept valid input without errors
Dropdown selections work correctly
Password field masks input for security
Confirmation password matches original password
Form submission succeeds without validation errors
Success page displays with message: 'PwD registration successful'
Confirmation includes both platform access mentions (AT/AD and SwarajAbility)
testCases.postconditions: PwD account is created in AT/AD system; Account is linked to SwarajAbility; Relationship is established between caregiver and PwD

testCases.title: Verify confirmation page displays dual-platform access messaging
testCases.preconditions: PwD registration form is completed and submitted successfully
steps: 1. Complete all steps of PwD registration as caregiver
2. Click 'Submit' or 'Register' button on final form
3. Wait for confirmation page to load
4. Read all messaging on confirmation page
5. Verify messaging includes AT/AD platform mention
6. Verify messaging includes SwarajAbility platform mention
7. Check for any links to platform documentation or help
8. Verify registration summary displays key information
9. Note any next steps provided to caregiver/PwD
expectedResults: Confirmation page displays immediately after submission
Success message clearly states: 'PwD Registration Successful'
Message explicitly mentions AT/AD platform access is granted
Message explicitly mentions SwarajAbility platform access is granted
Both platform names are visible and highlighted
Page includes credentials or links for accessing both platforms
Registration summary displays PwD name and registration date
Clear next steps are provided (e.g., 'PwD can now log in using credentials')
Option to return to dashboard or register another PwD is presented
testCases.postconditions: Caregiver understands dual-platform access has been granted; Clear pathway forward is established

testCases.title: Verify mandatory field validation in PwD registration form
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is registered and logged in; PwD registration form is accessible
steps: 1. Navigate to PwD registration form
2. Leave 'First Name' field empty and attempt to submit
3. Verify error message for missing first name
4. Clear form and leave 'Last Name' empty, attempt submit
5. Verify error message for missing last name
6. Clear form and leave 'Email' field empty, attempt submit
7. Verify error message for missing email
8. Clear form and leave 'Disability Type' unselected, attempt submit
9. Verify error message for missing disability type
10. Leave 'Password' field empty, attempt submit
11. Verify error message for missing password
12. Leave 'Confirm Password' empty, attempt submit
13. Verify error message for missing confirmation password
14. Fill all mandatory fields correctly and attempt submit
expectedResults: Each mandatory field produces specific error message if empty
Error messages are clear and actionable (e.g., 'First Name is required')
Error highlighting shows which field is problematic (red border or icon)
Form does not submit when any mandatory field is empty
All fields must be completed before successful submission
No partial registration occurs
testCases.postconditions: Form validation is confirmed to work correctly for all mandatory fields

testCases.title: Verify email format validation in PwD registration
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is registered and logged in; PwD registration form is accessible
steps: 1. Navigate to PwD registration form
2. Enter invalid email format: 'notanemail'
3. Attempt to submit form
4. Verify error message for invalid email format
5. Enter another invalid format: 'user@'
6. Attempt to submit
7. Verify error message
8. Enter format missing TLD: 'user@domain'
9. Attempt to submit and verify error
10. Enter valid email: 'rajesh.kumar@example.com'
11. Verify email is accepted
12. Try email with special characters: 'rajesh+kumar@example.com'
13. Verify if accepted (valid format with plus addressing)
expectedResults: Invalid email formats trigger error message: 'Please enter a valid email address'
Form prevents submission with invalid email
Standard valid email format is accepted (user@domain.tld)
Email with plus addressing is accepted (if supported)
Clear error message guides user to correct format
testCases.postconditions: Email validation is working correctly on form

testCases.title: Verify password strength requirements and validation
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is registered and logged in; PwD registration form is accessible
steps: 1. Navigate to PwD registration form
2. Locate password field and view any strength requirements shown
3. Enter weak password: '123456'
4. Check if system indicates password strength
5. Enter password without uppercase: 'password123'
6. Verify if validation error or warning appears
7. Enter password without special character: 'Password123'
8. Check validation status
9. Enter strong password: 'SecurePass@123'
10. Verify password is accepted
11. Verify password field masks characters entered
12. Check confirm password field and attempt mismatch
expectedResults: Password strength requirements are displayed to user
Weak passwords trigger error or warning message
Password must contain minimum 8 characters
Password must contain uppercase letters (A-Z)
Password must contain lowercase letters (a-z)
Password must contain numbers (0-9)
Password must contain special characters (@, #, $, etc.)
Strong passwords are accepted without error
Password field hides characters as they are typed
Confirm password field must match original password
testCases.postconditions: Password security requirements are validated and enforced

testCases.title: Verify duplicate email prevention (PwD email must be unique)
testCases.preconditions: First PwD has been registered with email 'rajesh.kumar@example.com'; Caregiver is logged in
steps: 1. Initiate registration for second PwD
2. Enter same email address: 'rajesh.kumar@example.com'
3. Fill in other form fields with different data
4. Attempt to submit form
5. Verify system prevents duplicate email registration
6. Check error message displayed to user
7. Change email to unique address: 'rajesh.kumar2@example.com'
8. Attempt form submission again
9. Verify form submission succeeds with unique email
expectedResults: System validates email uniqueness on form submission
Error message displays: 'This email address is already registered'
Form submission is blocked with duplicate email
User is prompted to enter different email
Unique email is accepted and registration proceeds
Each PwD has distinct email address in system
testCases.postconditions: Email uniqueness constraint is enforced; Duplicate email registration is prevented

testCases.title: Verify caregiver cannot register with same email as PwD
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is registered with email 'john.smith@example.com'; Caregiver has registered a PwD with email 'rajesh.kumar@example.com'
steps: 1. As caregiver, attempt to register another PwD
2. Try to use caregiver's own email in PwD email field: 'john.smith@example.com'
3. Fill remaining mandatory fields
4. Attempt to submit form
5. Verify system prevents using caregiver email for PwD
6. Check error message
7. Confirm same rule applies in reverse (PwD cannot use existing caregiver emails)
expectedResults: System validates that PwD email differs from caregiver email
Error message displays: 'This email is associated with an existing account'
Form submission is blocked
User is prompted to use different email address
Each user (caregiver and PwD) must have distinct email
testCases.postconditions: Email uniqueness across user types is enforced

testCases.title: Verify phone number format validation
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is registered and logged in; PwD registration form is accessible
steps: 1. Navigate to PwD registration form
2. Enter invalid phone format: 'abc123'
3. Attempt to submit form
4. Verify error message for invalid phone format
5. Enter phone with insufficient digits: '123'
6. Attempt to submit, verify error
7. Enter valid phone format: '9876543210'
8. Verify phone is accepted
9. Try phone with formatting: '+91-9876543210'
10. Verify if accepted (if supported by system)
expectedResults: Invalid phone formats trigger error: 'Please enter a valid phone number'
Phone field accepts 10-digit format (or applicable country format)
Phone field may accept formatted numbers with hyphens or country code
Non-numeric characters (except allowed formatting) are rejected
Valid phone numbers are accepted without error
testCases.postconditions: Phone number validation is working correctly

testCases.title: Verify successful registration of PwD with special characters in name
testCases.preconditions: Caregiver is registered and logged in; PwD registration form is accessible
steps: 1. Navigate to PwD registration form
2. Enter name with special characters: "O'Brien"
3. Verify name field accepts apostrophe
4. Enter name with hyphen: "Jean-Paul"
5. Verify name field accepts hyphen
6. Try name with accents: "José" or "François"
7. Verify name field accepts accented characters
8. Fill remaining mandatory fields
9. Submit form
10. Verify successful registration despite special characters in name
11. Log in as registered PwD and verify name displays correctly
expectedResults: Name field accepts apostrophes (e.g., O'Brien)
Name field accepts hyphens (e.g., Jean-Paul)
Name field accepts accented characters (e.g., José)
Registration succeeds with special characters in name
Name is stored and displayed correctly throughout system
No data corruption or character encoding issues occur
testCases.postconditions: Special character handling in names is verified to be correct

testCases.title: Verify accessibility compliance for PwD registration form (WCAG standards)
testCases.preconditions: PwD registration form is accessible; Accessibility testing tools available
steps: 1. Open PwD registration form
2. Run accessibility audit tool (WAVE, Axe, or similar)
3. Verify all form fields have proper labels
4. Check form labels are associated with input fields via 'for' attribute
5. Test keyboard navigation through entire form
6. Verify tab order is logical and sensible
7. Test form submission via keyboard (Enter key)
8. Test with screen reader (NVDA, JAWS, or VoiceOver)
9. Verify screen reader announces form fields, labels, and errors correctly
10. Check color contrast meets WCAG AA standards (4.5:1 for text)
11. Verify all interactive elements are keyboard accessible
12. Test error messages are announced by screen reader
expectedResults: No critical accessibility errors from audit tools
All form fields have associated labels
Keyboard navigation is fully functional through entire form
Tab key navigates through logical field order
Screen reader announces all fields, labels, and instructions
Error messages are announced and highlighted
Color contrast meets WCAG standards
Form is operable for users with assistive technologies
Form complies with WCAG 2.1 Level AA standards
testCases.postconditions: Accessibility compliance is verified for PwD registration form

AC1_caregiver_registration_required.description: PwD registration enabled only after caregiver registration complete

AC2_caregiver_consent_required.description: PwD registration requires explicit caregiver consent

AC3_pwd_profile_creation.description: Creates or links PwD account and enables SwarajAbility access

AC6_confirmation_messaging.description: Displays confirmation for both platform access

assumptions: Caregiver registration module is fully functional and tested
AT/AD platform database is accessible and operational
SwarajAbility platform is accessible and can receive authentication requests
Email verification is either disabled or mocked for testing
Test environment has sufficient data capacity for test records
Related bug SCRUM-726 may impact test execution of registration button responsiveness
Consent templates are finalized and displayed in UI
dependencies: Caregiver registration must be completed before PwD registration (AC1)
Consent management system must be integrated and functional
Database schema must support caregiver-PwD relationships
Backend API must support dual-platform account creation
Authentication system must support account linking for SwarajAbility
Related issue SCRUM-726 ('Register & Add PwD' button) must be resolved

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Are there specific accessibility requirements for this registration flow?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects test case TC_PWD_REG_012; May require WCAG compliance testing

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: What data fields are required vs. optional in PwD profile creation?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects validation test cases; Currently assuming standard fields are mandatory

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Is email verification required during registration or can it be done post-registration?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects test data setup and registration flow; Impacts test environment configuration

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: What happens if caregiver attempts to register PwD after caregiver account deactivation?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects edge case testing; Not currently covered in test plan

open_questions_and_clarifications.question: Is consent renewable/updatable by caregiver after PwD registration?
open_questions_and_clarifications.impact: Affects post-registration testing scope; May require additional test cases

priority_order: Highest tests (TC_PWD_REG_001, 002, 003) should be executed first
High priority tests follow Highest tests
Medium and Low priority tests can be executed in parallel
Accessibility tests (TC_PWD_REG_012) should be included in comprehensive test runs

estimated_effort.critical_path: 6-8 hours for critical path testing only

test_environment_requirements: Staging environment with AT/AD and SwarajAbility instances
Test database with pre-populated caregiver accounts
Email mock or sandbox for testing notifications
Network throttling tools for error condition testing
Multiple browser versions for cross-browser testing
Mobile device emulators or real devices for mobile testing
Admin access for database verification and security testing
known_issues: SCRUM-726: 'Register & Add PwD' button not responding after filling mandatory details - May block test execution
regression_areas: Caregiver registration flow (prerequisite for PwD registration)
User authentication and login for both AT/AD and SwarajAbility
Relationship management between caregiver and PwD

metadata.coverage: 100% of acceptance criteria covered with multiple scenarios per requirement
